title: DNP3 Control Relay Output Block (CROB) Tracking
app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884)
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:dnp3:json" block_type="Control_Relay_Output_Block"
| stats values(function_code) as phases values(operation_type) as ops values(trip_control_code) as trips latest(status_code) as last_status by _time source_h destination_h index_number uid
| table _time source_h destination_h index_number phases ops trips last_status
description: CROB select/operate sequences directly actuate breakers and outputs; a full audit trail is required for NERC CIP-style reviews and post-incident forensics.
value: CROB select/operate sequences directly actuate breakers and outputs; a full audit trail is required for NERC CIP-style reviews and post-incident forensics.
implementation: Enable ICSNPP-DNP3 control logging on Zeek sensors facing RTU/MTU paths. Ingest `dnp3_control` fields; map `index_number` to one-line diagrams. Require change correlation for OPERATE phases outside maintenance.
visualization: Table (full CROB audit), Timeline (SELECT vs OPERATE), Bar chart (operates by index_number).
knownFalsePositives: CROB and control events during remote operations drills, switchgear testing, or maintenance cutovers.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€˘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884).
â€˘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:dnp3:json"` (ICSNPP `dnp3_control.log`: `block_type`, `function_code`, `operation_type`, `index_number`, `trip_control_code`, `status_code`).
â€˘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 â€” Configure data collection
Enable ICSNPP-DNP3 control logging on Zeek sensors facing RTU/MTU paths. Ingest `dnp3_control` fields; map `index_number` to one-line diagrams. Require change correlation for OPERATE phases outside maintenance.

Step 2 â€” Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:dnp3:json" block_type="Control_Relay_Output_Block"
| stats values(function_code) as phases values(operation_type) as ops values(trip_control_code) as trips latest(status_code) as last_status by _time source_h destination_h index_number uid
| table _time source_h destination_h index_number phases ops trips last_status
```

Understanding this SPL

**DNP3 Control Relay Output Block (CROB) Tracking** â€” CROB select/operate sequences directly actuate breakers and outputs; a full audit trail is required for NERC CIP-style reviews and post-incident forensics.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:dnp3:json"` (ICSNPP `dnp3_control.log`: `block_type`, `function_code`, `operation_type`, `index_number`, `trip_control_code`, `status_code`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedâ€”rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: zeek:dnp3:json. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

â€˘ Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="zeek:dnp3:json". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
â€˘ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time source_h destination_h index_number uid** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
â€˘ Pipeline stage (see **DNP3 Control Relay Output Block (CROB) Tracking**): table _time source_h destination_h index_number phases ops trips last_status

Step 3 â€” Validate
Run the search for a short window and compare key fields with Zeek or Corelight protocol logs on the sensor for the same flow. Reconcile function codes, endpoints, and time order against a small known-good capture if needed.

Step 4 â€” Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (full CROB audit), Timeline (SELECT vs OPERATE), Bar chart (operates by index_number).
grandmaExplanation: We use deep packet inspection to track control relay and binary output actions so remote commands line up with what we expect.
